title: Hello Express API
shortDescription: Create your first Express server with a simple GET endpoint.
description: Build a basic Express server that responds to a GET request at the root path. This is your introduction to Express routing and response handling.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Create an Express server
- Implement a \`GET /\` route
- Return a JSON response with a welcome message
- Export the Express app (do NOT call \`app.listen()\`)
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about status code and response format.

title: Query Parameter Parser
shortDescription: Parse and validate URL query parameters.
description: Create an endpoint that accepts query parameters and returns them in a structured format. Learn how Express handles query strings and basic input validation.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Implement \`GET /greet\` endpoint
- Accept \`name\` and \`age\` as query parameters
- Return a personalized greeting
- Handle missing parameters gracefully
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

title: Request Body Parser
shortDescription: Handle POST requests and parse JSON request bodies.
description: Create a POST endpoint that accepts JSON data and processes it. Learn how to use Express middleware for parsing request bodies.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Implement \`POST /users\` endpoint
- Accept JSON request body with \`name\` and \`email\`
- Validate that both fields are present
- Return the created user with a generated ID
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

title: Custom Error Handler
shortDescription: Implement a global error handling middleware
description: Create a centralized error handler that catches and formats errors from all routes. Learn about Express error handling middleware and proper error responses.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Implement a global error handling middleware
- Format all errors consistently
- Handle both synchronous and asynchronous errors
- Return appropriate status codes
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

### Routes to Implement
- GET /success - Returns { message: "Success!" }
- GET /error - Throws an error to test error handler

### Implementation Hints
- Error middleware has 4 parameters: (err, req, res, next)
- Place error handler AFTER all routes
- Use res.status() to set appropriate status codes

title: Path Parameters
shortDescription: Extract and use URL path parameters in routes.
description: Create RESTful routes that use path parameters to identify resources. Learn how to work with dynamic route segments in Express.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Implement GET /users/:id endpoint
- Extract the id from the URL path
- Return user information based on the ID
- Handle invalid IDs appropriately
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

### Additional Challenge
- Implement GET /posts/:postId/comments/:commentId to practice nested path parameters.
constraints: Use req.params to access path parameters

title: Request Logger Middleware
shortDescription: Create custom middleware to log HTTP requests
description: Build a logging middleware that captures request details including method, URL, timestamp, and response time. Learn about middleware execution order and request/response cycle.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Create a custom logging middleware
- Log: method, URL, timestamp, and response time
- Attach logs to req.logs array for testing
- Apply middleware globally to all routes
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

### Log Format
Each log entry should contain:

{
  "method": "GET",
  "url": "/api/users",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-28T10:30:00.000Z",
  "responseTime": 15
}

### Routes to Implement
- GET /logs - Return all captured logs
- GET /api/users - Sample route (returns \`{ users: [] }\`)
- POST /api/users - Sample route (returns \`{ created: true }\`)

### Hints
- Use Date.now() to measure response time
- Attach logs array to app.locals.logs
- Middleware runs before route handlers

title: In-Memory CRUD API
shortDescription: Build a complete CRUD API with in-memory storage.
description: Implement a RESTful API for managing tasks with full CRUD operations using an in-memory array. Learn REST conventions and proper HTTP status codes.
instructions:
### Requirements
Implement these endpoints:

1. POST /tasks - Create a new task
2. GET /tasks - Get all tasks
3. GET /tasks/:id - Get a specific task
4. PUT /tasks/:id - Update a task
5. DELETE /tasks/:id - Delete a task

### Status Codes
- 201 - Created (POST)
- 200 - Success (GET, PUT, DELETE)
- 404 - Not Found
- 400 - Bad Request (missing fields)

See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

title: JWT Authentication Middleware
description: Create authentication middleware that verifies JWT tokens and protects routes. Learn industry-standard authentication patterns used in modern web applications.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Implement POST /login endpoint (generates JWT)
- Create authentication middleware
- Protect GET /profile route with middleware
- Use Authorization: Bearer <token> header
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

### Error Responses
- No token: 401 {"error": "No token provided"}
- Invalid token: 401 {"error": "Invalid token"}

### Environment Variables
- JWT_SECRET - Secret key for signing/verifying tokens

title: Input Validation Middleware
shortDescription: Create reusable validation middleware for request data.
description: Build a validation system that checks request bodies, query parameters, and path parameters. Learn how to sanitize and validate user input to prevent security vulnerabilities.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Create a validation middleware factory
- Validate email format, required fields, and data types
- Support validation for body, query, and params
- Return clear validation error messages
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

### Routes to Implement

1. POST /register - Validate user registration
   - Required: \`email\`, \`password\`, \`name\`
   - Email must be valid format
   - Password min 8 characters
   - Name must be string, 2-50 chars

2. GET /search - Validate query params
   - \`query\` (required, min 3 chars)
   - \`limit\` (optional, number, max 100)

### Successful Response (200/201)
Return the validated data
constraints: Support email, string length, number validation

title: File Upload Handler
shortDescription: Handle multipart file uploads with validation.
description: Implement file upload functionality with size limits, type validation, and proper error handling. Learn how to work with multipart/form-data in Express.

title: API Versioning
shortDescription: Implement API versioning using route prefixes.
description: Create multiple versions of an API endpoint to support backward compatibility. Learn best practices for evolving APIs without breaking existing clients.
instructions:
### Requirements
Implement two versions of a users API:

**Version 1 (\`/v1/users\`)**
{
  "id": 1,
  "fullName": "John Doe"
}

**Version 2 (\`/v2/users\`)**
{
  "id": 1,
  "firstName": "John",
  "lastName": "Doe",
  "email": "john@example.com"
}

### Routes to Implement
- GET /v1/users - Returns v1 format
- GET /v1/users/:id - Returns single user (v1)
- GET /v2/users - Returns v2 format
- GET /v2/users/:id - Returns single user (v2)

### Implementation Approach
- Use Express Router for each version
- Create separate route handlers
- Share data layer (in-memory array)
- Transform data based on version

### Hint
const v1Router = express.Router();

const v2Router = express.Router();

app.use('/v1', v1Router);

app.use('/v2', v2Router);

See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

title: CORS Configuration
shortDescription: Implement custom CORS middleware with origin whitelisting.
description: Configure Cross-Origin Resource Sharing to control which domains can access your API. Learn about browser security and modern web application architecture.
instructions:
### Requirements
- Create custom CORS middleware (no packages)
- Whitelist specific origins
- Handle preflight (OPTIONS) requests
- Support credentials
- See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

### Allowed Origins
[
  'http://localhost:3000',
  'https://myapp.com',
  'https://www.myapp.com'
]

### CORS Headers to Set
- Access-Control-Allow-Origin
- Access-Control-Allow-Methods
- Access-Control-Allow-Headers
- Access-Control-Allow-Credentials

### Routes to Implement
- GET /api/public - Public endpoint
- POST /api/data - Protected endpoint
- Handle \`OPTIONS\` for all routes

### Response for Blocked Origin (403)
{
  "error": "CORS policy: Origin not allowed"
}

### Hints
- Check req.headers.origin
- Set headers using res.set()

title: JWT with Refresh Tokens
shortDescription: Implement JWT authentication with access and refresh tokens.
description: Build a complete authentication system with short-lived access tokens and long-lived refresh tokens. Learn modern authentication best practices used in production.
instructions:
### Requirements
Implement a dual-token authentication system

**Access Token**: Short-lived (15 minutes)
**Refresh Token**: Long-lived (7 days)

### Endpoints to Implement

1. POST /auth/login
   - Accept email/password
   - Return both tokens

2. POST /auth/refresh
   - Accept refresh token
   - Return new access token

3. GET /auth/profile
   - Protected with access token
   - Return user profile

4. POST /auth/logout
   - Invalidate refresh token

### Login Response (200)
{
  "accessToken": "eyJhbG...",
  "refreshToken": "eyJhbG...",
  "expiresIn": 900
}

### Token Storage
- Store refresh tokens in-memory Map
- Associate with user ID
- Check validity on refresh

### Security Requirements
- Access tokens expire in 15 minutes
- Refresh tokens expire in 7 days
- Invalidated tokens cannot be reused
- Return 401 for expired/invalid tokens

See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format

title: GraphQL-like Query API
shortDescription: Build a flexible query API with field selection and filtering.
description: Create an API that supports GraphQL-like field selection, allowing clients to request only the data they need. Learn advanced query parsing and response transformation.
instructions:
### Requirements
Implement a REST endpoint with query-based field selection

### Query Syntax
- GET /api/users?fields=id,name,email
- GET /api/users?fields=id,name&filter=verified:true
- GET /api/users/123?fields=name,posts.title

### Features to Support

1. **Field Selection**
   - ?fields=id,name - Return only specified fields
   - No fields param - Return all fields

2. **Filtering**
   - ?filter=verified:true
   - ?filter=age>25
   - ?filter=name:John

3. **Nested Selection** (bonus)
   - ?fields=id,posts.title,posts.date

### Example
Request: GET /api/users?fields=id,name&filter=verified:true

Response:
[
  { "id": 1, "name": "John" },
  { "id": 2, "name": "Jane" }
]

See Example Usage section, to get more idea about Status codes and Response format
